@@ -727,7 +727,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-wf7z","title":"Surface non-fatal auto-flush failures as operator-visible sync debt","description":"Lens: Operational reliability

Finding: Mutating commands can return success even when the automatic JSONL export fails. The failure is currently logged as non-fatal debug/tracing in several paths, so an operator or agent may commit stale JSONL without seeing command-output evidence that sync debt exists. This is especially confusing because auto-flush is the default behavior.

Evidence:
- `src/main.rs` logs `Auto-flush failed (non-fatal)` at debug level after command success.
- Routed mutation commands log similar non-fatal routed auto-flush failures.
- Troubleshooting docs cover explicit sync failures, but not a successful mutation whose background/default auto-flush failed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Human output or JSON/robot output surfaces auto-flush failure state after a successful mutation, or records durable `needs_flush`/sync-debt state that `doctor`/status reports.
- Docs include a short operator runbook for recovering from non-fatal auto-flush failure.
- Regression coverage verifies a mutation cannot silently leave stale JSONL without an operator-visible signal.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"Codex4","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:06:23.354171489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:21:48.495463708Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:21:48.495076652Z","close_reason":"Surfaced post-mutation auto-flush export failures to stderr with structured AUTO_FLUSH_FAILED diagnostics for JSON/robot modes, reused the warning across local and routed mutation paths, documented the recovery runbook, and added an e2e regression","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","auto-flush","observability","operational"],"comments":[{"id":276,"issue_id":"beads_rust-wf7z","author":"MistyDune","text":"degraded-coordination: Agent Mail health is red; intended files: src/main.rs plus focused regression test/docs if needed. Avoiding AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/AGENT_INTEGRATION.md, docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md because b0gt is in progress there.","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:02:11Z"},{"id":277,"issue_id":"beads_rust-wf7z","author":"MistyDune","text":"Paused before code changes per orchestrator redirect to beads_rust-1jop; returning this bead to open so it remains visible.","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:02:41Z"},{"id":280,"issue_id":"beads_rust-wf7z","author":"Codex4","text":"degraded-coordination: Agent Mail health is red; intended files: src/main.rs, tests/e2e_errors.rs or focused auto-flush regression test, docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md if runbook docs are needed","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:07:34Z"},{"id":281,"issue_id":"beads_rust-wf7z","author":"Codex4","text":"degraded-coordination update: expanding intended files to src/main.rs, src/cli/commands/{mod.rs,create.rs,update.rs,delete.rs,close.rs,reopen.rs,defer.rs,dep.rs,comments.rs,label.rs}, tests/e2e_errors.rs, docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:10:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-wmkh","title":"Stabilize dataset_registry source_commit detection under RCH/offloaded test runs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T03:09:37.962956376Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:22:20.217617227Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:22:20.217104947Z","close_reason":"Stabilized dataset source_commit detection under RCH and CI/offloaded test environments","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-wo9u","title":"[MEDIUM] Export leaks comment rowid ordering into JSONL","description":"Root cause: src/sync/mod.rs normalizes each issue before attaching comments in both file export (lines 1654-1666) and writer export (lines 1915-1927). normalize_issue_for_export has canonical comment sorting (lines 2377-2385), but comments populated from storage bypass it and are serialized in database id order. Repro: create an issue with equal-timestamp comments inserted in non-canonical author/body order, export JSONL, and observe the comments follow SQLite rowid order rather than the canonical export comparator. Proposed fix: attach comments before normalize_issue_for_export in both export paths so labels, dependencies, and comments are normalized together. Test to add: regression in jsonl import/export coverage asserting same-timestamp comments export sorted by author/body/id, not insertion rowid.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_2","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:33:47.092594928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:43:26.725104301Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:43:26.724795914Z","close_reason":"Fixed by loading comments before export normalization in both JSONL export paths and adding a same-timestamp comment ordering regression. Verification notes: cargo fuzz merge_issue completed 20000 runs without crash; rustfmt --edition 2024 --check passed on changed files; UBS passed on changed files. Full cargo test/check/clippy were attempted through rch but are currently blocked by unrelated /data/projects/frankensqlite fsqlite-planner AccessPath::probe compile errors and an untracked repro_sync_cycle_fuzz_crashes.rs fmt issue.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":301,"issue_id":"beads_rust-wo9u","author":"cod_2","text":"degraded-coordination: files: src/sync/mod.rs, tests/jsonl_import_export.rs","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:34:01Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-ws3g","title":"[MEDIUM] Rust and Go content_hash algorithms diverge","description":"Rust br and Go bd do not compute the same content_hash for the same canonical issue, so cross-tool sync can miss content-based dedup/collision matches.

Trace:
- Rust implementation: src/util/hash.rs:93-107 writes title/description/design/acceptance_criteria/notes/status/priority/type/assignee/owner/created_by/external_ref/source_system/pinned/is_template with NUL separators. Priority is rendered as P{n}; booleans are rendered as true/false.
- Go bd implementation: legacy_beads/internal/types/types.go:130-203 writes the same early fields with NUL separators, but priority is decimal {n}; booleans are empty when false and pinned/template when true; it also hashes additional bd fields after is_template.
- tests/conformance.rs normalize_value masks content_hash to NORMALIZED_HASH, so live br-vs-bd conformance does not catch this. tests/storage_id_hash_parity.rs has determinism/sensitivity fixtures but no fixed Go digest vector.

Concrete example:
Issue content: title=Test, description/design/acceptance_criteria/notes absent or empty, status=open, priority=2, issue_type=task, assignee/owner/created_by/external_ref/source_system absent, pinned=false, is_template=false, no labels/deps/comments. Timestamps and id are ignored.
Rust byte fields: Test, '', '', '', '', open, P2, task, '', '', '', '', '', false, false -> SHA256 4aaae9b8f8be7873ad6ba64b575b070d10d433d8362b95ebdb5fbe085c5ef03f.
Go byte fields per legacy_beads/internal/types/types.go default Issue: Test, '', '', '', '', open, 2, task, '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', 0, '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '' -> SHA256 7e838ad83dcd637681e6c3f334f2cbb6556c912420fa373ab6af8ff65eed78fc.

Expected fix: decide the canonical cross-tool content hash spec, update Rust/Go or migration behavior accordingly, and add a conformance/golden test that compares a fixed canonical digest instead of masking content_hash.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane6","created_at":"2026-04-22T23:56:28.991178158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:02:33.245438880Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","hash","sync"],"comments":[{"id":357,"issue_id":"beads_rust-ws3g","author":"pane6","text":"degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable; intended files: src/util/hash.rs, src/model/mod.rs, tests/conformance*.rs, tests/proptest_hash.rs, and storage migration files only if content_hash rebuild requires it","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:02:33Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-ws3g","title":"[MEDIUM] Rust and Go content_hash algorithms diverge","description":"Rust br and Go bd do not compute the same content_hash for the same canonical issue, so cross-tool sync can miss content-based dedup/collision matches.

Trace:
- Rust implementation: src/util/hash.rs:93-107 writes title/description/design/acceptance_criteria/notes/status/priority/type/assignee/owner/created_by/external_ref/source_system/pinned/is_template with NUL separators. Priority is rendered as P{n}; booleans are rendered as true/false.
- Go bd implementation: legacy_beads/internal/types/types.go:130-203 writes the same early fields with NUL separators, but priority is decimal {n}; booleans are empty when false and pinned/template when true; it also hashes additional bd fields after is_template.
- tests/conformance.rs normalize_value masks content_hash to NORMALIZED_HASH, so live br-vs-bd conformance does not catch this. tests/storage_id_hash_parity.rs has determinism/sensitivity fixtures but no fixed Go digest vector.

Concrete example:
Issue content: title=Test, description/design/acceptance_criteria/notes absent or empty, status=open, priority=2, issue_type=task, assignee/owner/created_by/external_ref/source_system absent, pinned=false, is_template=false, no labels/deps/comments. Timestamps and id are ignored.
Rust byte fields: Test, '', '', '', '', open, P2, task, '', '', '', '', '', false, false -> SHA256 4aaae9b8f8be7873ad6ba64b575b070d10d433d8362b95ebdb5fbe085c5ef03f.
Go byte fields per legacy_beads/internal/types/types.go default Issue: Test, '', '', '', '', open, 2, task, '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', 0, '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '', '' -> SHA256 7e838ad83dcd637681e6c3f334f2cbb6556c912420fa373ab6af8ff65eed78fc.

Expected fix: decide the canonical cross-tool content hash spec, update Rust/Go or migration behavior accordingly, and add a conformance/golden test that compares a fixed canonical digest instead of masking content_hash.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane6","created_at":"2026-04-22T23:56:28.991178158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:20:50.894158967Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T00:20:50.893784826Z","close_reason":"Aligned Rust content_hash with Go bd reference, added fixed Go digest and proptest coverage, and added schema v7 migration to rebuild stored content_hash values. Implementation commits: 0d8fd47, f9b2bbd, b225425; clippy follow-up: 0bd1cbb.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","hash","sync"],"comments":[{"id":357,"issue_id":"beads_rust-ws3g","author":"pane6","text":"degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable; intended files: src/util/hash.rs, src/model/mod.rs, tests/conformance*.rs, tests/proptest_hash.rs, and storage migration files only if content_hash rebuild requires it","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:02:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-wyr","title":"Unit test coverage expansion (no mocks)","description":"# Unit Coverage Expansion

## Scope
- Add/expand unit tests across core modules without mocks/fakes.
- Use real SQLite (TempDir + .beads) and real JSONL files.

## Requirements
- Deterministic test data and timestamps.
- Avoid network access and external dependencies.
- Prefer table-driven tests to capture edge cases.

## Acceptance
- All unit suites pass via cargo test.
- New tests include edge cases + error paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T16:16:41.959059236Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:25.448907681Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:25.448907681Z","close_reason":"Fixed failing tests: added sync.* prefix to is_startup_key function, fixed determine_action test calls with missing 4th argument. All 243 library tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
